concerns.text: FR-006 and US-3 explicitly mandate a Generalized Additive Model (GAM) as the PRIMARY method for threshold detection, with segmented regression as a fallback. Tasks T030 and T031 reverse this, prioritizing a 'Parametric 1/sqrt(k) model' as primary and demoting GAM to secondary. This leaves the primary requirement (FR-006) unimplemented as specified.

FR-001 requires acquiring a corpus of at least 50 real-world meta-analyses. T012 instructs an immediate fallback to simulation if download fails, and T014 implements the simulation. There is no task to explicitly measure, report, or validate the success of the real-world acquisition attempt against the ≥50 target (SC-001) before falling back, risking a silent failure of the primary data path.

SC-003 and FR-007 require identifying the study count where coverage stabilizes within ±2% of the nominal target. T033 reads this target from `config.py`, but no task exists to define, document, or set this 'deferred' value in `config.py` or the spec. The task is unimplementable without a defined constant.

T014 in Phase 2 is described as 'Implement chunked processing logic' (a prerequisite for FR-002/US-1 data acquisition), but T014 in Phase 3 is described as 'Implement simulation fallback'. The same ID (T014) is used for two distinct, unrelated tasks, creating ambiguity and potential implementation gaps for the chunked processing requirement.

Task T014 in Phase 3 is labeled as implementing the 'simulation fallback' but is also assigned to 'chunked processing logic' in its description. This conflates two distinct concerns (data acquisition fallback vs. memory management) into a single task, violating the separation of concerns required for independent testing of the data path.
